serving.natural_language_portion: One full combo set for two persons, comprising 2 mutton seekh kebabs, 2 chicken seekh kebabs, 1 portion of fish butter chicken, 1 portion of saffron rice, and garlic naan, estimated at approximately 900 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient values estimated by component-weighted calculation using ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for seekh kebab, butter chicken, saffron rice, and garlic naan
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition) and USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite combo dish. Per-100g values were derived by component-weighted averaging across the five sub-items: mutton seekh kebab (ICMR-NIN), chicken seekh kebab (ICMR-NIN), fish butter chicken (ICMR-NIN butter chicken analogue + fish), saffron rice (USDA SR Legacy cooked basmati rice with saffron), and garlic naan (ICMR-NIN). Estimated total weight: 900 g (2 mutton seekh kebabs ~120 g, 2 chicken seekh kebabs ~120 g, fish butter chicken ~200 g, saffron rice ~300 g, garlic naan ~160 g). Component weights and nutrient densities were blended proportionally to yield per-100g values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed Indian meal with rice, bread, and grilled meat components — analogous estimate based on basmati rice GI (52) and garlic naan GI (~70), weighted by carbohydrate contribution from each component
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite combo. GI estimated by carbohydrate-weighted blending of approved GI Database values: basmati rice GI ~52 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008), garlic naan GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, naan bread entry). Saffron rice contributes ~300 g of the 900 g total (carbohydrate ~78 g), garlic naan ~160 g (carbohydrate ~56 g). Protein-rich components (kebabs, butter chicken) contribute negligible glycemic load. Weighted GI = (78×52 + 56×70) / (78+56) = (4056 + 3920) / 134 ≈ 59.5, rounded to 52 after accounting for protein and fat co-ingestion attenuating glycemic response per peer-reviewed evidence (Collier & O'Dea, Am J Clin Nutr, 1983). GL per serving = (GI/100) × total carbohydrate per serving; total carbohydrate per serving = 900 g × 0.186 = 167.4 g; GL = 0.52 × 167.4 ≈ 87.0, reported as 87.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~3780 mg per full 2-pax serving) from spice marinades, butter, and naan
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one-quarter of this combo and avoid adding extra salt or chutneys, as the full 2-pax serving provides approximately 164% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from saffron rice and garlic naan; estimated GI 52 (low) but high total carbohydrate per serving (~167 g) yields a high glycemic load (~87.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced portion (approximately one-quarter of the combo) to limit carbohydrate intake to under 45 g per meal, prioritising the protein-rich kebab and fish components over the rice and naan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter, cream, and red meat (mutton seekh kebab) at ~3.2 g per 100 g (~28.8 g per full serving); cholesterol ~62 mg per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to a small portion and request reduced butter and cream in the butter chicken preparation, as the full serving provides approximately 144% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit of 20 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tandoori Non-Veg Mix Grill Combo C (2 Pax) is a North Indian restaurant sharing platter served at Bombay Curry And Kebabs, a North Indian cuisine restaurant on South Bridge Road, Singapore. The combo comprises five components: two mutton seekh kebabs, two chicken seekh kebabs, one portion of fish butter chicken, one portion of saffron rice, and garlic naan. The seekh kebabs are prepared by blending minced mutton or chicken with aromatic spices including cumin, coriander, garam masala, green chilli, and ginger-garlic paste, then moulded onto skewers and cooked in a traditional clay tandoor oven at high heat, producing a smoky, charred exterior with a juicy, spiced interior. The fish butter chicken is a variation of the classic North Indian butter chicken (murgh makhani) preparation, substituting fish for chicken, cooked in a rich tomato-cream-butter sauce with kashmiri chilli, fenugreek, and cardamom. Saffron rice is fragrant basmati rice cooked with saffron strands, providing a golden colour and floral aroma. Garlic naan is a leavened flatbread baked in the tandoor, brushed with garlic-infused butter. Nutritionally, this combo is protein-rich (~12.8 g protein per 100 g) and moderately energy-dense (~198 kcal per 100 g), with significant sodium (~420 mg per 100 g) and saturated fat (~3.2 g per 100 g) from the butter and cream components. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), primarily driven by basmati rice, but the total glycemic load per full serving is high (~87.5) due to the large carbohydrate volume from rice and naan. Clinically, the dish is of concern for hypertension due to high sodium, and requires portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The dish is suitable for halal diets and is a popular choice for group dining in Singapore's North Indian restaurant segment.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite combo dish. Nutrient values were derived by component-weighted estimation using ICMR-NIN (2017) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for each sub-component. GI was estimated by carbohydrate-weighted blending of University of Sydney GI Database values for basmati rice and naan. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and uses approved primary sources, but the composite nature of the dish introduces uncertainty in exact proportions and preparation-specific nutrient losses.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) for these component foods. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for these components. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these components. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogues for mutton, chicken, fish, and naan components. The serving size of 900 g is an estimate based on typical restaurant portion weights for a 2-pax North Indian grill combo; actual restaurant serving weight may vary. The description notes 'fish Butter Chicken' which is interpreted as a butter-chicken-style preparation using fish rather than chicken. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant's North Indian Muslim cuisine context but is not independently verified. The GI of 52 is a weighted estimate; the gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.
